s3.ptxt: Disconnect the cooling fan ECU connector.
Turn the ignition switch on (IG). Check the voltage of the +B terminal of the disconnected wire harness side connector.
If the result is not as specified, inspect the power source system (fusible link, fuse, wire harness and relay).

s2.ptxt: Inspect the cooling fan motor (see page ).
Measure the resistance between terminals RFC (ECM) and SI (cooling fan ECU) of the wire harness side connectors.

item.ptxt: If the fan does not operate, there may be a short circuit.
If the fan remains operating, there may be an open circuit.

s2.ptxt: Inspect the ECM power source circuit and ground circuit.
Inspect the input signal and output current.

atten3.ptxt: Be sure to perform the inspection with the radiator coolant temperature less than 95°C (203 °F).

s3.ptxt: Connect the 400 A probe of the ammeter to terminal M+ of the cooling fan motor.
Set the intelligent tester to the oscilloscope function.
Using the intelligent tester, check the waveform between terminals RFC and E1 of the ECM.

item.ptxt: If the input signal is abnormal, there is a malfunction in the ECM or cooling fan ECU.
If the output current is abnormal even when the input signal is normal, there is a malfunction in the cooling fan ECU (RFC) or motor.
